description: Implement the spanning-tree pointer surgery that swaps the entering arc in and the leaving arc out.
commit: feat: add tree-structure update after pivot
steps: Create tree.go in the repo root (SPDX BSL-1.0, package mcf).

Implement `func (s *solverState) updateTreeStructure(entering, leaving, joinNode int, deltaDirFirst bool) (subtreeRoot int, potentialDelta int64)`. Mirror LEMON's `updateTreeStructure` in network_simplex.h exactly. The function returns the root of the subtree whose potentials need adjusting and the signed delta (positive or negative) to apply — 05a (updatePotentials) consumes these.

Implementation outline (port LEMON directly, this is the high-risk area — the agent MUST open lemon/network_simplex.h and port the updateTreeStructure routine line-by-line rather than re-derive it): (a) determine u_in/v_in/u_out/v_out from entering and leaving plus the deltaDirFirst flag; (b) detach the subtree rooted at u_out from its current parent in the thread, revThread, succNum, lastSucc arrays; (c) reverse the path from u_in up to the subtree root that must be re-rooted, flipping parent/predArc/dirNode along the way; (d) splice the detached subtree beneath v_in in the thread; (e) update succNum and lastSucc along the path from v_in to the original joinNode.

Also flip arc states: s.state[leaving] becomes stateLower or stateUpper depending on which cycle side saturated (if the leaving arc's residual in cycle direction was forward-cap the new state is stateUpper, otherwise stateLower); s.state[entering] becomes stateTree.

`potentialDelta` equals the change in the entering arc's reduced cost sign times its violation: specifically, for an entering arc at stateLower with reducedCost rc (negative and making violation positive), the subtree that becomes newly attached gets potential shift `-rc` on the v_in side (LEMON's formula: `delta = -reducedCost(entering)` for lower-entering, `+reducedCost(entering)` for upper-entering). Document the exact sign by citing LEMON's updatePotential block.
Subtree root returned is the node on the v_in side that is the root of the newly-attached subtree after re-linking (LEMON names this `u_in` after the re-root swap, then walks potentials from there).
As in LEMON, carefully maintain thread/revThread such that the thread remains a valid Euler-tour traversal of the new tree.

The fetch step the agent should actually run (put this in the steps literally): `mkdir -p /tmp/lemon-ref && cd /tmp/lemon-ref && curl -sfL https://lemon.cs.elte.hu/pub/sources/lemon-1.3.1.tar.gz -o lemon-1.3.1.tar.gz && tar xzf lemon-1.3.1.tar.gz && echo 'Reference extracted at /tmp/lemon-ref/lemon-1.3.1/lemon/network_simplex.h'`. Use that file as the authoritative source for this port.

Create tree_test.go. Build a small 5-node tree (plus root=5) by hand where entering and leaving arcs are known. TestUpdateTreeStructureMaintainsParentChain: after updateTreeStructure, every non-root node must still reach the root via s.parent in at most n steps (walk up; assert you hit the root). Assert s.state[entering] == stateTree and s.state[leaving] != stateTree. Assert succNum[root] == n+1 is preserved. TestUpdateTreeStructureThreadIsEulerTour: after update, starting at root and following s.thread must visit every node exactly once before returning to root.
acceptance_criteria: updateTreeStructure is ported from LEMON 1.3.1 network_simplex.h's updateTreeStructure routine (cite the LEMON function in a code comment referencing the line range in the extracted source).
After a call: every node reaches root via parent pointers; thread forms a valid Euler tour; state[entering]==stateTree; state[leaving] is stateLower or stateUpper; succNum[root] == n+1.
